methods.description: This method describes the preparation of hazelnut trees for pollination experiments, including emasculation, enclosure, and pollination procedures to study pollen tube growth and desiccation effects.

steps.description: Hazelnut trees were emasculated and enclosed in cages (2.4 × 2.4 × 2.4 m) that were covered with white polyethylene on 13 Dec. 1991 to prevent pollination.
steps.searchable_summary: emasculate and enclose hazelnut trees in cages to prevent pollination

steps.description: Branches bearing female flowers were excised from caged trees, brought to the greenhouse in plastic bags, recut under water, and kept in flasks with the cut ends in water.
steps.searchable_summary: excise branches with female flowers and prepare them in the greenhouse

steps.description: Branches were divided into three groups: pollinated with compatible pollen, pollinated with incompatible pollen, and control (touched but not pollinated).
steps.searchable_summary: divide branches into groups for pollination experiments

steps.description: Flowers were sampled at various intervals from 2 h up to 28 h after pollination.

methods.description: This method outlines the fixation, dehydration, and electron microscopy procedures for examining hazelnut flowers, including critical-point drying and coating for SEM analysis.

steps.description: Flowers were placed in microporous specimen containers and fixed in FAA at room temperature for at least 2 h.
Samples were dehydrated in 50%, 70%, and 100% water-acetone solutions, followed by 50%, 70%, and 100% acetonetrichlorotrifluoroethane solutions.
Samples were critical-point dried with monochlorotrifluoromethane in a dryer and coated with gold-palladium alloy in a vacuum evaporator.
steps.searchable_summary: critical-point dry and coat samples for SEM

steps.description: The flowers were viewed on a scanning electron microscope (SEM) operated at 7 or 10 kV.

methods.description: This method describes the examination of pollen tube growth in hazelnut styles using aniline blue dye and fluorescence microscopy.

steps.description: Styles were squashed in aniline blue dye and examined with a fluorescence microscope equipped with an ultraviolet light source.
steps.searchable_summary: examine styles for pollen tube growth using fluorescence microscopy

methods.description: This method outlines a preliminary experiment to compare fresh and fixed sample appearance and assess the impact of ambient conditions on stylar desiccation.

steps.description: Some styles were examined before pollination and within 1 h of excision.
Pollinated and unpollinated styles were examined after 2 h at ambient conditions with or without being covered with plastic bags.
steps.searchable_summary: examine styles under ambient conditions with/without plastic cover

steps.description: Electron microscopy was performed as outlined previously, except that samples were observed at 5 kV.
